ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cpu_models" (
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPU model */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the CPU model */,
  "cpuid" INTEGER /* CPUID of the CPU model */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the CPU model (e.g., Production, Discontinued) */,
  "launch_date" TEXT /* Launch date of the CPU model */,
  "core_count" INTEGER /* Number of cores in the CPU model */,
  "thread_count" INTEGER /* Number of threads in the CPU model */,
  "clock_speed" REAL /* Clock speed of the CPU model */,
  "cache_size" INTEGER /* Cache size of the CPU model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_models_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES manufacturing_status ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mcus" (
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MCU */,
  "mcu_name" TEXT /* Name of the MCU */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the MCU */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Firmware version of the MCU */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_mcu_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU model */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the MCU */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., Production, Affected) */,
  "relationship_status" TEXT /* Status of the relationship (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_mcu_relationships_cpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("cpu_id") REFERENCES cpu_models ("cpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_mcu_relationships_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES mcus ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "manufacturing_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Production, Discontinued) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the type (e.g., Broadwell DE, Broadwell E) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  "architecture" TEXT /* Architecture of the type (e.g., x86, ARM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_cpu_type_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU model */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_cpu_type_relationships_cpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("cpu_id") REFERENCES cpu_models ("cpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_cpu_type_relationships_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES cpu_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU model being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the CPU model */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the CPU model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_cpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("cpu_id") REFERENCES cpu_models ("cpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data included in the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., CPU production, CPU type) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the CPU model related to the notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., CPU update, CPU alert) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_cpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("cpu_id") REFERENCES cpu_models ("cpu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "notification_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of notifications (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "report_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of reports (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
